response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship, TDD, and distributed-systems exposure, but the role is explicitly C++ centric and requires deep STL, multi-threaded systems, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity experience. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments roughly a decade ago, with the bulk of recent work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. No evidence of industrial automation, OPC UA / Modbus / IEC protocols, or SDL practices, making this a poor technical fit despite a strong general engineering profile.
strengths: Broad senior engineering background with TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship rigor
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput, networked, multi-threaded systems (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Demonstrated mentoring and technical communication via workshops, talks, and courses
concerns: Only ~6 months of actual C/C++ work, dated to 2014–2015 at National Instruments
No evidence of IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity / OPC UA / Modbus / MQTT experience
No industrial automation or IoT-platform domain exposure; recent stack is JS/TS-centric

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443/SDL) and automation protocols
Strong general senior engineering and distributed-systems track record
